channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: What movies are best to see on the Big Screen
item.description: NPR staff debate the best movies to see on the big screen.
item.content:encoded: NPR staff debate the best movies to see on the big screen.
item.dc:creator: Marc Rivers

item.title: K-pop group BTS returns with an epic live concert in Seoul
item.description: The K-pop band BTS returns with a live concert in Seoul after an almost four-year absence while its members were doing military service.
item.content:encoded: The K-pop band BTS returns with a live concert in Seoul after an almost four-year absence while its members were doing military service.
item.dc:creator: Adrian Ma

item.title: Italy's far-right Prime Minister's political future at stake in referendum vote
item.description: Italian Prime Minister's close relationship with President Trump may weigh on a referendum on reforming Italy's judiciary.
item.content:encoded: Italian Prime Minister's close relationship with President Trump may weigh on a referendum on reforming Italy's judiciary.
item.dc:creator: Megan Williams

item.title: NATO allies prepare for a Russian attack in the far North
item.description: Despite tensions, U.S. and other NATO nations conduct exercises in the far North off Norway to prepare for Russian attack.
item.content:encoded: Despite tensions, U.S. and other NATO nations conduct exercises in the far North off Norway to prepare for Russian attack.
item.dc:creator: Teri Schultz

item.title: Israel's war strategy involves high-level assassinations. But will it win the war?
item.description: The war between the US, Israel and Iran is intensifying. NPR's Adrian Ma speaks with Yossi Melman, who reports on Israeli intelligence, about the state of the conflict as it enters its fourth week.
item.content:encoded: The war between the US, Israel and Iran is intensifying. NPR's Adrian Ma speaks with Yossi Melman, who reports on Israeli intelligence, about the state of the conflict as it enters its fourth week.
item.dc:creator: Adrian Ma

item.title: What a team of investigative reporters found out about Banksy's identity
item.description: A Reuters investigative team discovered intriguing details about Banksy's identity after seeing his art pop up in Ukraine.
item.content:encoded: A Reuters investigative team discovered intriguing details about Banksy's identity after seeing his art pop up in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Adrian Ma

item.title: What the 'Catching the Codfather' podcast found out about a fishing tycoon
item.description: NPR's Adrian Ma speaks with Ian Coss, host of GBH's 'Catching the Codfather' podcast, about fishing tycoon Carlos Rafael of New Bedford, Massachusetts.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Adrian Ma speaks with Ian Coss, host of GBH's 'Catching the Codfather' podcast, about fishing tycoon Carlos Rafael of New Bedford, Massachusetts.
item.dc:creator: Mallory Yu

item.title: ICE agents to be deployed to US airports beginning Monday
item.description: The president says he will send ICE agents to airports starting Monday. NPR's Luke Garrett unpacks what we know about the deployment.
item.content:encoded: The president says he will send ICE agents to airports starting Monday. NPR's Luke Garrett unpacks what we know about the deployment.
item.dc:creator: Adrian Ma

item.title: EV technology is developing quickly. What's it like to cover such a dynamic industry?
item.dc:creator: Camila Domonoske

item.title: How the Iran war is wasting American resources
item.dc:creator: Adrian Ma
